decisions.title: Prioritize Canvas disposition workflow over Test Results sidebar enhancements
decisions.context: Canvas disposition history/comments (ENG-9828) and Pass/Fail buttons (ENG-9437) are core to 'Nail the Core' strategy for Canvas product. Test Results sidebar improvements (ENG-9365, ENG-9507) are incremental UX polish. Need to sequence work to deliver decision-capture capabilities first.

arguments.for: Canvas disposition features directly drive North Star metric (Decisions Captured). Enables users to make and document pass/fail decisions inline. Critical for Q2 Canvas adoption targets.
arguments.against: Test Results improvements affect larger existing user base. Sidebar UX issues create friction in current workflow. Delaying may impact user satisfaction scores.

decisions.title: Approve ingest scale infrastructure investments before Q2 customer onboarding
decisions.context: Multiple ingest scalability issues surfacing: S3 throttling (ENG-9805), metadata loading failures at 65k+ entries (ENG-9837), load testing needed (ENG-9778). Three enterprise customers scheduled for April onboarding with expected 10x data volume.
decisions.blockedBy: Pending capacity planning review and budget approval for S3 throughput increase

arguments.for: Customer onboarding at risk without infrastructure improvements. Failed ingests create poor first impression and churn risk. Fixes support 'Embed Deeper' by handling enterprise-scale data.
arguments.against: Significant infrastructure cost increase (~$40k/mo estimated). May be premature optimization if customer volumes don't materialize. Could defer 2-3 weeks without immediate impact.

decisions.title: Fast-track SCIM user management blockers vs. new enterprise SSO features
decisions.context: Two critical SCIM blockers identified: email invitation flow (ENG-9785) and admin deactivation (ENG-9784) must be resolved. These block 4 enterprise deals requiring SCIM provisioning. Competes with Keycloak external sync work (ENG-9838).

arguments.for: SCIM blockers directly gate $800k+ in Q2 enterprise ARR. Security/compliance requirement for large customers. Supports 'Embed Deeper' by removing deployment friction.
arguments.against: Keycloak work enables broader SSO ecosystem (6+ identity providers). Larger TAM impact. SCIM workarounds exist via direct API management.

decisions.title: Commit to alignment workflow redesign for Family data products
decisions.context: Multiple alignment issues across products: auto-naming (ENG-9832), multiple alignment points (DES-61), dynamic window alignment (DES-134), Family data replacement (ENG-7289). Signals indicate current alignment UX is fragmented and confusing users.

arguments.for: Alignment is foundational to Signal>Evidence>Decision workflow. Poor UX creates friction in evidence gathering. Unified design supports 'Nail the Core' across Explore V2 and Canvas.
arguments.against: Large design investment with unclear ROI. Current system works for power users. May distract from Canvas MVP and Python Rules launches. Could phase improvements incrementally.

decisions.title: Resource Python Rules launch vs. Canvas rule-based reports feature
decisions.context: Canvas Output rule-based reports (DES-140) overlaps with Python Rules product positioning. Both enable programmatic decision logic. Team capacity insufficient to deliver both in Q2.

arguments.for: Python Rules is strategic 'Build the Moat' investment - enables customer-specific logic and extensibility. Opens advanced analytics use cases. Rule-based Canvas reports can follow in Q3.
arguments.against: Canvas reports serve broader user base (analysts vs. engineers). Lower technical barrier to adoption. Delivers faster time-to-value for existing Canvas users.

keyResults.name: Explore V2 time-series compare modes shipped

timeAudit.weekSummary: Meeting load 80% - above sustainable threshold. Three decision-forcing meetings scheduled but may lack sufficient prep time given limited focus blocks. Recommend declining Canvas rule-based reports kickoff (Fri) to create space for ingest scalability decision research.
alerts: Focus time only 20% of week - below 40% target for strategic work
Back-to-back 1:1s today (10:30-11:45) without buffer - risk of running over
No dedicated time blocked for Q2 planning due April 15
Five open decisions with deadlines in next 3 weeks - need prioritization
